2604.15866.title: DiZiNER: Disagreement-guided Instruction Refinement via Pilot Annotation Simulation for Zero-shot Named Entity Recognition
2604.15866.paper_summary: Large language models (LLMs) have advanced information extraction (IE) by enabling zero-shot and few-shot named entity recognition (NER), yet their generative outputs still show persistent and systematic errors. Despite progress through instruction fine-tuning, zero-shot NER still lags far behind supervised systems. These recurring errors mirror inconsistencies observed in early-stage human annotation processes that resolve disagreements through pilot annotation. Motivated by this analogy, we introduce DiZiNER (Disagreement-guided Instruction Refinement via Pilot Annotation Simulation for Zero-shot Named Entity Recognition), a framework that simulates the pilot annotation process, employing LLMs to act as both annotators and supervisors. Multiple heterogeneous LLMs annotate shared texts, and a supervisor model analyzes inter-model disagreements to refine task instructions. Across 18 benchmarks, DiZiNER achieves zero-shot SOTA results on 14 datasets, improving prior bests by +8.0 F1 and reducing the zero-shot to supervised gap by over +11 points. It also consistently outperforms its supervisor, GPT-5 mini, indicating that improvements stem from disagreement-guided instruction refinement rather than model capacity. Pairwise agreement between models shows a strong correlation with NER performance, further supporting this finding.
2604.15866.paper_summary_zh: 大型語言模型（LLMs）透過實現零樣本和少樣本的命名實體識別（NER）推進了信息提取（IE），然而它們的生成輸出仍然顯示出持續且系統性的錯誤。儘管通過指令微調取得了一些進展，零樣本 NER 仍然遠遠落後於監督系統。這些反覆出現的錯誤反映了在早期人類標註過程中觀察到的不一致性，這些不一致性通過試點標註來解決。受到這一類比的啟發，我們提出了 DiZiNER（通過試點標註模擬進行的分歧引導指令精煉以實現零樣本命名實體識別），這是一個模擬試點標註過程的框架，利用 LLM 作為標註者和監督者。多個異質 LLM 對共享文本進行標註，監督模型分析模型之間的分歧以精煉任務指令。在 18 個基準測試中，DiZiNER 在 14 個數據集上實現了零樣本 SOTA 結果，將先前最佳結果提高了 +8.0 F1，並將零樣本與監督之間的差距縮小了超過 +11 點。它還始終超越其監督者 GPT-5 mini，這表明改進源於分歧引導的指令精煉，而非模型能力。模型之間的成對一致性與 NER 性能之間顯示出強相關性，進一步支持了這一發現。
2604.15866.author: Siun Kim et.al.
